title: A Starless Planet With a Warm Hidden Ocean

beats.narration_text: med davy A-I. From Sanskrit — meaning intelligent, or intellectually brilliant. The perfect name for our A-I-powered intelligent learning systems.

props.headline: Medhavy AI
props.body:
मेधावी (Medhavy): From Sanskrit, meaning
"intelligent" or "intellectually brilliant"
— the perfect name for our AI-powered
intelligent learning systems.

beats.narration_text: A planet flung out of its star system should be a frozen tomb — no sunlight, no warmth. Yet the same physics that keeps Europa's ocean liquid could keep a subglacial ocean alive on a rogue planet, in permanent darkness, forever.

card.copy: A Starless Planet With a Warm Hidden Ocean

beats.narration_text: The energy budget of a planet: E-total = E-star plus E-core. E-star is sunlight absorbed. E-core is heat from radioactive decay in the planet's interior plus tidal flexing. For Earth, E-star is 99.97% of the budget. E-core is 0.03%.

production_viz.label: E_total = E_star + E_core. Earth: E_star=99.97%, E_core=0.03%

beats.narration_text: A rogue planet — ejected from its star system by a gravitational encounter — has E-star = 0. On Earth, that 0.03% from the core seems negligible. For a rogue, that 0.03% is 100% of the budget. Can it keep a subsurface ocean liquid?

card.copy: E_total = E_star + E_core. Rogue planet: E_star = 0. Earth's core provides 47 TW. Is 47 TW enough to keep a subsurface ocean liquid?

beats.narration_text: Earth's core provides 47 terawatts of heat — from radioactive decay of uranium, thorium, and potassium, plus residual heat from formation. This is the same heat that drives plate tectonics, volcanic activity, and hydrothermal vents on the ocean floor.

production_viz.label: Earth core: 47 TW from U, Th, K decay + formation heat. Drives tectonics + vents.

beats.narration_text: Jupiter's moon Europa has no significant core heating — but Jupiter's tidal forces flex the moon rhythmically, generating roughly 1 terawatt of heat. That 1 terawatt keeps a 100-kilometer deep ocean liquid under 30 kilometers of ice. Europa's ocean is the best candidate for life in our solar system.

production_viz.label: Europa: ~1 TW tidal → 100 km deep ocean under 30 km ice. Life candidate.

beats.narration_text: A rogue planet with a large captured moon would have similar tidal heating — potentially comparable to or exceeding Europa's. Plus core radiogenic heat. Total: potentially tens of terawatts of usable heat. A subglacial ocean could remain liquid for billions of years.

production_viz.label: Rogue + captured moon: tidal + radiogenic = tens of TW. Ocean for billions of years.

beats.narration_text: The temperature at a rogue planet's surface: minus 240 degrees Celsius — close to absolute zero. The temperature at the bottom of a thick ice layer: potentially above 0 degrees Celsius, depending on the heat flux. The ice cap is the insulator. The thicker the ice, the better the insulation for the liquid layer below.

production_viz.label: Surface: −240°C. Bottom of thick ice: potentially >0°C. Ice = insulator.

beats.narration_text: Earth's hydrothermal vents already demonstrate this principle. Black smoker ecosystems at the mid-ocean ridges receive zero sunlight — they run entirely on chemical energy from the Earth's interior. Entire ecosystems: tube worms, shrimp, microbes, all living on geothermal chemistry, no photosynthesis.

production_viz.label: Black smokers: zero sunlight. Full ecosystem on geothermal chemistry. Proven.

beats.narration_text: How many rogue planets are there? Current estimates: one or two rogue planets for every star in the Milky Way. That is hundreds of billions of rogue planets in our galaxy alone. If a fraction have liquid subsurface oceans, the number of potentially habitable environments in the galaxy just expanded enormously.

production_viz.label: ~1–2 rogue planets per star. Milky Way: hundreds of billions of rogues.

beats.narration_text: E = E-star plus E-core. Rogue: E-star = 0, but E-core = 47 terawatts for an Earth analog. Plus tidal heating from a moon. Subglacial ocean possible. Black smoker ecosystems prove the principle. Hundreds of billions of rogue planets in the Milky Way.

card.copy: E_core = 47 TW. Tidal heating possible with a moon. Subglacial ocean: liquid for billions of years. Black smokers prove the principle. Rogue planets: billions in the Milky Way.

beats.narration_text: med davy. A-I-powered intelligent learning systems. On YouTube, at med davy A-I. med davy dot com.
